mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.0%, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending capacity, which reduces corporate revenue expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0%, or the signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.01 during periods of rising unemployment.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in existing home sales and housing starts indicate robust household balance sheets and strong residential demand, which amplifies consumer discretionary spending and generates positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when RSAFS and HOUST both rise, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant with p-value above 0.10 in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production growth signals expanding manufacturing capacity and rising corporate output, which directly boosts short-term earnings revisions and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration yields negative or flat forward returns during easing periods, or the mechanism predictive accuracy drops below random chance with Rank IC below -0.01 in this regime.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth reflects stronger aggregate economic activity and corporate profit expansion, which systematically drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth shows no correlation with 60-day forward returns, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 across all tested periods.

mechanism_narrative: When monthly inflation accelerates beyond 0.4%, it triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which raises borrowing costs and compresses equity multiples, resulting in negative forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX returns remain positive in more than 60% of months where CPIAUCSL rises above 0.4%, or the inflation threshold fails to predict return direction with statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls combined with expanding industrial production indicates synchronized labor and output growth, which strengthens corporate earnings forecasts and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined signal produces negative forward returns when both series rise, or the interaction effect vanishes in walk-forward validation with p-value above 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Declining existing home sales signal weakening consumer confidence and reduced household leverage capacity, which dampens near-term economic momentum and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Falling RSAFS coincides with positive 5-day forward returns in more than 50% of observations, or the signal predictive power is indistinguishable from noise with Rank IC below 0.01 during late-cycle phases.
